sentences.sentence: Some of the senses that we and other terrestrial mammals take for granted are either reduced or absent in cetaceans or fail to function well in water.
sentences.translation: 我们和其他陆生哺乳动物习以为常的一些感官，在鲸类动物身上要么减弱了，要么不存在，或者在水中无法很好地发挥作用。

structure.text: that we and other terrestrial mammals take for granted
are either reduced or absent in cetaceans
or fail to function well in water

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 Some of the senses，说明这些感官是人类和陆生哺乳动物习以为常的。
either ... or ... 与后面的 or fail to ... 共同列出鲸类感官的三种可能状态。
terrestrial mammals 与 cetaceans 形成对比，突出从陆地到水中环境带来的感官差异。

sentences.sentence: For example, it appears from their brain structure that toothed species are unable to smell.
sentences.translation: 例如，从它们的大脑结构来看，齿鲸类物种似乎没有嗅觉。

structure.text: that toothed species are unable to smell

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正的信息由 that 引导的从句说明。
from their brain structure 表示判断依据，即研究者是根据大脑结构作出推断。
toothed species 指齿鲸类物种，不是泛指所有鲸类。

sentences.sentence: Baleen species, on the other hand, appear to have some related brain structures but it is not known whether these are functional.
sentences.translation: 另一方面，须鲸类物种似乎有一些相关的大脑结构，但人们尚不清楚这些结构是否具有功能。

structure.text: appear to have some related brain structures

difficult_points: on the other hand 将须鲸与上一句的齿鲸形成对比。
appear to have 表示谨慎推断，而不是确定结论。
whether 引导的从句说明未知内容；these 指代前面的 related brain structures。

sentences.sentence: It has been speculated that, as the blowholes evolved and migrated to the top of the head, the neural pathways serving sense of smell may have been nearly all sacrificed.
sentences.translation: 有人推测，随着呼吸孔进化并迁移到头顶，负责嗅觉的神经通路可能几乎全部被牺牲掉了。

structure.text: that, as the blowholes evolved and migrated to the top of the head, the neural pathways serving sense of smell may have been nearly all sacrificed
as the blowholes evolved and migrated to the top of the head
the neural pathways serving sense of smell
may have been nearly all sacrificed

difficult_points: It has been speculated 是被动表达，表示“有人推测”，后面的 that 从句才是推测内容。
as 引导时间状语从句，说明呼吸孔演化迁移的同时发生了神经通路的变化。
serving sense of smell 是现在分词短语，修饰 the neural pathways。
may have been sacrificed 是情态动词加完成被动结构，表示对过去变化的推测。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, although at least some cetaceans have taste buds, the nerves serving these have degenerated or are rudimentary.
sentences.translation: 同样，尽管至少有一些鲸类动物拥有味蕾，但支配这些味蕾的神经已经退化，或者还很原始。

structure.text: although at least some cetaceans have taste buds

difficult_points: although 引导让步状语从句，承认有些鲸类有味蕾，但强调相关神经功能不足。
serving these 修饰 nerves，these 指代前面的 taste buds。
have degenerated 和 are rudimentary 是并列谓语，分别表示已经退化和发育原始。

expressions.meaning_zh: 处于原始状态；发育不完全

sentences.sentence: The sense of touch has sometimes been described as weak too, but this view is probably mistaken.
sentences.translation: 触觉有时也被描述为薄弱，但这种看法很可能是错误的。

structure.text: has sometimes been described as weak too
but this view is probably mistaken

difficult_points: has been described as 是现在完成时的被动结构，表示这种说法曾经存在。
too 承接上一段关于嗅觉和味觉减弱的讨论。
this view 指“鲸类触觉很弱”这一看法，而不是指触觉本身。

sentences.sentence: Trainers of captive dolphins and small whales often remark on their animals' responsiveness to being touched or rubbed, and both captive and free-ranging cetacean individuals of all species (particularly adults and calves, or members of the same subgroup) appear to make frequent contact.
sentences.translation: 圈养海豚和小型鲸的训练员常常提到，它们对被触摸或摩擦反应灵敏；而且各个物种中无论是圈养还是自由活动的鲸类个体，尤其是成年个体和幼崽，或同一小群体的成员，似乎都会频繁接触。

structure.text: Trainers of captive dolphins and small whales
often remark on their animals' responsiveness to being touched or rubbed
and both captive and free-ranging cetacean individuals of all species (particularly adults and calves, or members of the same subgroup) appear to make frequent contact
particularly adults and calves, or members of the same subgroup

difficult_points: Trainers of captive dolphins and small whales 是第一分句的主语，of 短语限定训练员的对象。
responsiveness to 后接 being touched or rubbed，表示对被触碰或摩擦的反应能力。
both captive and free-ranging 修饰 cetacean individuals，说明圈养和野外个体都包括在内。
括号内容进一步说明 frequent contact 常见于成年个体、幼崽或同一小群体成员之间。

sentences.sentence: This contact may help to maintain order within a group, and stroking or touching are part of the courtship ritual in most species.
sentences.translation: 这种接触可能有助于维持群体内部的秩序，而在大多数物种中，抚摸或触碰也是求偶仪式的一部分。

structure.text: may help to maintain order within a group
and stroking or touching are part of the courtship ritual in most species

difficult_points: This contact 回指上一句中鲸类个体之间的 frequent contact。
may help to maintain 表示可能的功能，而不是已经被绝对证明的作用。
stroking or touching 是动名词并列结构，整体作第二分句的主语。
courtship ritual 对应求偶行为，说明触觉也参与繁殖相关行为。

sentences.sentence: The area around the blowhole is also particularly sensitive and captive animals often object strongly to being touched there.
sentences.translation: 呼吸孔周围的区域也格外敏感，圈养动物常常强烈抗拒那里被触碰。

structure.text: and captive animals often object strongly to being touched there

difficult_points: around the blowhole 修饰 The area，说明敏感区域的位置。
and 连接两个相关事实：该区域敏感，以及动物不愿被碰那里。
object to 后接动名词形式 being touched，there 指呼吸孔周围区域。

sentences.sentence: The sense of vision is developed to different degrees in different species.
sentences.translation: 不同物种的视觉发育程度各不相同。
difficult_points: is developed 是被动形式，这里描述视觉这一感官的发育状态。
to different degrees 表示程度差异，是理解本段比较不同物种视觉能力的关键。
in different species 限定比较范围，即不同鲸类或相关物种之间。

sentences.sentence: Baleen species studied at close quarters underwater - specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii - have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater, and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air.
sentences.translation: 研究者曾在水下近距离研究须鲸类物种——具体包括一只圈养了一年的灰鲸幼崽，以及在阿根廷和夏威夷近海被研究和拍摄的自由活动的露脊鲸和座头鲸——这些动物显然能在水下用视觉追踪物体，而且它们在水中和空气中似乎都能看得还算不错。

structure.text: specifically a grey whale calf in captivity for a year, and free-ranging right whales and humpback whales studied and filmed off Argentina and Hawaii
have obviously tracked objects with vision underwater
and they can apparently see moderately well both in water and in air
both in water and in air

difficult_points: studied at close quarters underwater 是过去分词短语，修饰 Baleen species。
两个破折号之间的内容是具体例子，说明被近距离研究的须鲸包括哪些。
they 指代 Baleen species，而不是只指其中某一个例子。
both in water and in air 表示须鲸在水中和空气中都有一定视觉能力。

sentences.sentence: However, the position of the eyes so restricts the field of vision in baleen whales that they probably do not have stereoscopic vision.
sentences.translation: 然而，须鲸眼睛的位置极大限制了它们的视野，因此它们很可能没有立体视觉。

structure.text: so restricts the field of vision in baleen whales
that they probably do not have stereoscopic vision

difficult_points: However 引出转折：须鲸能看见，但视觉范围仍受限制。
so ... that ... 构成结果结构，表示限制严重到导致缺乏立体视觉。
they 指代 baleen whales；stereoscopic vision 指双眼形成深度感的立体视觉。

sentences.sentence: On the other hand, the position of the eyes in most dolphins and porpoises suggests that they have stereoscopic vision forward and downward.
sentences.translation: 另一方面，大多数海豚和鼠海豚的眼睛位置表明，它们拥有向前和向下的立体视觉。

structure.text: the position of the eyes in most dolphins and porpoises
that they have stereoscopic vision forward and downward

difficult_points: On the other hand 将海豚、鼠海豚与前一段的须鲸进行对比。
主语是 the position of the eyes，谓语 suggests 表示从眼睛位置作出的推断。
that 引导宾语从句；they 指 most dolphins and porpoises。

sentences.sentence: Eye position in freshwater dolphins, which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding, suggests that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward.
sentences.translation: 淡水海豚的眼睛位置表明，它们所拥有的视觉是向前和向上的立体视觉；这些海豚进食时常常侧身游动或倒着游。

structure.text: which often swim on their side or upside down while feeding
that what vision they have is stereoscopic forward and upward

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 freshwater dolphins 的游泳方式。
while feeding 是省略结构，相当于 while they are feeding。
what vision they have 表示“它们所拥有的视觉”，作 that 从句中的主语。
forward and upward 说明立体视觉的方向，与上一句的 forward and downward 对比。

sentences.sentence: By comparison, the bottlenose dolphin has extremely keen vision in water.
sentences.translation: 相比之下，宽吻海豚在水中拥有极其敏锐的视觉。
difficult_points: By comparison 表示与前面其他海豚或须鲸的视觉能力进行比较。
the bottlenose dolphin 用单数加定冠词指代这一类动物。
keen vision 表示敏锐的视力，不是“热衷的视觉”。

sentences.sentence: Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish, it can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well.
sentences.translation: 从它观察并追踪空中飞鱼的方式来看，它显然也能相当清楚地透过空气与水的交界面看东西。

structure.text: Judging from the way it watches and tracks airborne flying fish
it watches and tracks airborne flying fish
can apparently see fairly well through the air-water interface as well

difficult_points: Judging from ... 引出推断依据，即通过观察宽吻海豚追踪飞鱼的方式来判断。
两个 it 都指代上一句的 the bottlenose dolphin。
through the air-water interface 表示透过空气和水的界面，是理解 from under the water 的关键。
as well 表示它不仅在水中视力好，也能透过界面看得较好。

sentences.sentence: And although preliminary experimental evidence suggests that their in-air vision is poor, the accuracy with which dolphins leap high to take small fish out of a trainer's hand provides anecdotal evidence to the contrary.
sentences.translation: 而且，尽管初步实验性证据表明它们在空气中的视觉较差，但海豚高高跃起从训练员手中叼走小鱼的准确性，却提供了与此相反的轶事证据。

structure.text: although preliminary experimental evidence suggests that their in-air vision is poor
that their in-air vision is poor
the accuracy with which dolphins leap high to take small fish out of a trainer's hand
with which dolphins leap high to take small fish out of a trainer's hand
to take small fish out of a trainer's hand
provides anecdotal evidence to the contrary

difficult_points: although 引导让步状语从句，先承认实验初步证据显示空气中视觉差。
the accuracy with which ... 是主句的长主语，核心名词是 accuracy。
to take small fish out of a trainer's hand 表示跃起的目的。
to the contrary 指与“空气中视觉差”这一说法相反。

sentences.sentence: Such variation can no doubt be explained with reference to the habitats in which individual species have developed.
sentences.translation: 这种差异无疑可以参照各个物种演化发展的栖息地来解释。

structure.text: in which individual species have developed

difficult_points: Such variation 回指前文不同物种视觉能力和视野方向的差异。
can ... be explained 是情态动词加被动结构，说明这些差异可以被解释。
in which 引导定语从句，修饰 habitats，相当于 individual species have developed in the habitats。

sentences.sentence: For example, vision is obviously more useful to species inhabiting clear open waters than to those living in turbid rivers and flooded plains.
sentences.translation: 例如，相比生活在浑浊河流和泛滥平原中的物种，视觉显然对栖息在清澈开阔水域中的物种更有用。

structure.text: is obviously more useful to species inhabiting clear open waters than to those living in turbid rivers and flooded plains
living in turbid rivers and flooded plains

difficult_points: more useful ... than ... 构成比较结构，比较视觉对两类栖息地物种的价值。
inhabiting clear open waters 是现在分词短语，修饰 species。
those 指代 species，living in turbid rivers and flooded plains 修饰 those。
clear open waters 与 turbid rivers and flooded plains 形成环境对比。

sentences.sentence: The South American boutu and Chinese beiji, for instance, appear to have very limited vision, and the Indian susus are blind, their eyes reduced to slits that probably allow them to sense only the direction and intensity of light.
sentences.translation: 例如，南美的 boto 和中国的白鱀豚似乎视觉非常有限，而印度恒河豚则是盲的，它们的眼睛退化成细缝，大概只能感知光线的方向和强度。

structure.text: The South American boutu and Chinese beiji
appear to have very limited vision
and the Indian susus are blind
that probably allow them to sense only the direction and intensity of light

difficult_points: for instance 插入句中，表明这些物种是前一句栖息地影响视觉的例子。
appear to have 表示推测性的描述，而非绝对断言。
their eyes reduced to slits 是独立主格结构，补充说明 Indian susus 失明的状态。
that 引导定语从句修饰 slits，them 指 Indian susus。

sentences.sentence: Although the senses of taste and smell appear to have deteriorated, and vision in water appears to be uncertain, such weaknesses are more than compensated for by cetaceans' well-developed acoustic sense.
sentences.translation: 尽管味觉和嗅觉似乎已经退化，水中的视觉也似乎并不确定，但这些弱点被鲸类发达的听觉远远弥补了。

structure.text: Although the senses of taste and smell appear to have deteriorated, and vision in water appears to be uncertain
the senses of taste and smell
are more than compensated for by cetaceans' well-developed acoustic sense

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，其中 taste and smell 与 vision in water 是两个并列的弱项。
such weaknesses 回指前面提到的味觉、嗅觉退化以及水中视觉不确定。
more than compensated for 表示“不只是弥补，而是充分弥补”。
by cetaceans' well-developed acoustic sense 说明弥补这些弱点的是发达的听觉。

sentences.sentence: Most species are highly vocal, although they vary in the range of sounds they produce, and many forage for food using echolocation①.
sentences.translation: 大多数物种都非常善于发声，尽管它们发出的声音范围各不相同，而且许多物种会利用回声定位觅食①。

structure.text: although they vary in the range of sounds they produce
and many forage for food using echolocation①

difficult_points: highly vocal 表示这些动物会频繁或大量发声，不是指“嗓门高”。
although 从句说明不同物种发声范围有差异，与 Most species are highly vocal 形成补充。
sounds they produce 中省略了关系词 that，修饰 sounds。
using echolocation① 是现在分词短语，说明 forage for food 的方式。

expressions.meaning_zh: 高度发声的；善于发声的

sentences.sentence: Large baleen whales primarily use the lower frequencies and are often limited in their repertoire.
sentences.translation: 大型须鲸主要使用较低频率的声音，而且它们能发出的声音种类往往有限。

structure.text: and are often limited in their repertoire

difficult_points: primarily 表示“主要地”，说明大型须鲸使用声音频率的主要范围。
and are often limited 与 primarily use 共用主语 Large baleen whales。
repertoire 原指可表演的全部曲目，这里指鲸类能发出的声音种类或范围。

sentences.sentence: Notable exceptions are the nearly song-like choruses of bowhead whales in summer and the complex, haunting utterances of the humpback whales.
sentences.translation: 显著的例外包括弓头鲸在夏季发出的近似歌声的合唱，以及座头鲸复杂而令人难忘的声音。

structure.text: the nearly song-like choruses of bowhead whales in summer and the complex, haunting utterances of the humpback whales

difficult_points: Notable exceptions 指前一句“大型须鲸声音种类通常有限”的例外情况。
主句是主系表结构，表语由两个并列名词短语构成。
nearly song-like choruses 和 complex, haunting utterances 分别描述弓头鲸和座头鲸的声音特点。

sentences.sentence: Toothed species in general employ more of the frequency spectrum, and produce a wider variety of sounds, than baleen species (though the sperm whale apparently produces a monotonous series of high-energy clicks and little else).
sentences.translation: 总体而言，齿鲸类比须鲸类使用更多的频率范围，并能发出更丰富多样的声音（不过抹香鲸显然只发出一连串单调的高能量咔嗒声，几乎没有别的声音）。

structure.text: employ more of the frequency spectrum, and produce a wider variety of sounds, than baleen species
though the sperm whale apparently produces a monotonous series of high-energy clicks and little else
produces a monotonous series of high-energy clicks and little else

difficult_points: in general 限定整体趋势，后面括号中的 sperm whale 是例外。
more ... and a wider variety ... than ... 构成比较结构，比较齿鲸和须鲸的发声范围。
括号中的 though 引导让步从句，说明抹香鲸不像其他齿鲸那样声音丰富。
little else 表示“几乎没有其他东西”，这里指几乎没有其他声音。

sentences.sentence: Some of the more complicated sounds are clearly communicative, although what role they may play in the social life and "culture" of cetaceans has been more the subject of wild speculation than of solid science.
sentences.translation: 一些更复杂的声音显然具有交流功能，不过它们在鲸类的社会生活和“文化”中可能扮演什么角色，更多还是疯狂猜测的对象，而不是严谨科学研究的结论。

structure.text: Some of the more complicated sounds
although what role they may play in the social life and "culture" of cetaceans has been more the subject of wild speculation than of solid science
what role they may play in the social life and "culture" of cetaceans
has been more the subject of wild speculation than of solid science

difficult_points: although 引导让步状语从句，承认声音有交流功能，但其社会文化作用并不确定。
what role they may play ... 是名词性从句，在 although 从句中作主语。
引号中的 "culture" 表明作者对鲸类是否具有类似人类文化的概念保持谨慎。
more ... than ... 对比 wild speculation 和 solid science，说明证据仍不充分。

expressions.meaning_zh: 大胆猜测；缺乏根据的推测

sentences.sentence: ① Echolocation: the perception of objects by means of sound wave echoes.
sentences.translation: ① 回声定位：通过声波回声感知物体。

structure.text: the perception of objects by means of sound wave echoes
by means of sound wave echoes

difficult_points: 句首的 ① 表明这是对前文 echolocation 的脚注解释。
冒号后面的名词短语给出定义，而不是一个完整谓语句。
by means of 表示方式，说明回声定位依靠声波回声来感知物体。
